variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

historicEvents.description: Charles I of Spain became Emperor Charles V of the Holy Roman Empire.

en.lifeSummary: When Ieuan ap Rhys was born about 1430, in Llandyssul, Cardiganshire, Wales, his father, Rhys Goch ap Llywelyn, was 36 and his mother, Jonet ferch Maredudd, was 32. He had at least 1 daughter with Mawd ferch Ieuan.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ieuan ap Rhys est né environ 1430, à Llandyssul, Cardiganshire, Galles, son père, Rhys Goch ap Llywelyn, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Jonet ferch Maredudd, avait 32 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Mawd ferch Ieuan.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ieuan ap Rhys um 1430 in Llandyssul, Cardiganshire, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Rhys Goch ap Llywelyn 36 und seine Mutter Jonet ferch Maredudd 32. Er hatte mit Mawd ferch Ieuan mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ieuan ap Rhys è nato il circa 1430 nel Llandyssul, Cardiganshire, Galles, il suo padre, Rhys Goch ap Llywelyn, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Jonet ferch Maredudd, aveva 32 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Mawd ferch Ieuan.

ja.lifeSummary: Ieuan ap Rhysが約1430年にウェールズカーディガンシュアイアランディスルで生まれた時，父Rhys Goch ap Llywelynは36歳，母Jonet ferch Mareduddは32歳であった。彼はMawd ferch Ieuanとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Ieuan ap Rhys(이)가 대략 1430년 웨일스 카디건실르 랜디설에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Rhys Goch ap Llywelyn(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Jonet ferch Maredudd(은)는 32세였다. 그는 Mawd ferch Ieuan(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ieuan ap Rhys nasceu aproximadamente 1430, em Llandyssul, Cardiganshire, Gales, seu pai, Rhys Goch ap Llywelyn, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Jonet ferch Maredudd, tinha 32 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Mawd ferch Ieuan.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ieuan ap Rhys родился около 1430 г. в местности лландиссул, кардиганьшир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Rhys Goch ap Llywelyn было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Jonet ferch Maredudd, 32. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Mawd ferch Ieuan.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ieuan ap Rhys nació aproximadamente 1430, en Llandyssul, Cardiganshire, Gales, su papá, Rhys Goch ap Llywelyn, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Jonet ferch Maredudd, tenía 32 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Mawd ferch Ieuan.
